rules.title: Future Tense Formation - Basic Structure
rules.text: Slovak has two ways to form the future tense: 1) For PERFECTIVE verbs, use the present tense conjugation (which has future meaning). 2) For IMPERFECTIVE verbs, use the future auxiliary 'budem' (will be) + infinitive.
examples: Prečítam knihu – I will read the book (perfective, present form = future meaning)
Budem čítať knihu – I will be reading the book (imperfective, auxiliary + infinitive)
Napíšem list – I will write a letter (perfective)

rules.title: Future Tense - Perfective Verbs
rules.text: Perfective verbs use their present tense conjugation to express future meaning. They describe completed actions that will happen in the future.
examples: Urobím to zajtra – I will do it tomorrow
Napíšeš mi email? – Will you write me an email?
Prečítame knihu – We will read the book

rules.title: Future Auxiliary 'byť' - Conjugation
rules.text: The future auxiliary 'budem' (I will be) is used with imperfective infinitives to form the future tense.
examples: Budem pracovať – I will be working
Budeš študovať? – Will you be studying?
Budeme čakať – We will be waiting

rules.title: Future Tense - Imperfective Verbs
rules.text: Imperfective verbs form the future with 'budem' + infinitive. This describes ongoing or repeated actions in the future.
examples: Budem robiť domácu úlohu – I will be doing homework
Budeš pracovať zajtra? – Will you be working tomorrow?
Budú čítať celý deň – They will be reading all day

rules.title: Aspect in Future Tense - Perfective vs Imperfective
rules.text: Choose perfective for completed future actions with a clear endpoint. Choose imperfective for ongoing, repeated, or habitual future actions.
examples: Budem čítať knihu (ongoing process)
Prečítam knihu (will finish reading it)
Budeme pracovať celý deň (duration)
Odpracujeme osem hodín (completion)

rules.title: Reflexive Verbs in Future Tense - Perfective
rules.text: Perfective reflexive verbs use present tense conjugation + reflexive particle 'sa/si' for future meaning. The reflexive particle comes after the verb.
examples: Naučím sa slovenčinu – I will learn Slovak
Umyješ sa ráno? – Will you wash in the morning?
Oblečieme sa rýchlo – We will get dressed quickly

rules.title: Reflexive Verbs in Future Tense - Imperfective
rules.text: Imperfective reflexive verbs use 'budem' + reflexive particle + infinitive. The reflexive particle comes between the auxiliary and infinitive.
examples: Budem sa učiť celý deň – I will be learning all day
Budeš sa umývať večer? – Will you be washing in the evening?
Budú sa obliekať pomaly – They will be getting dressed slowly

rules.title: Negation in Future Tense - Perfective Verbs
rules.text: Add 'ne-' prefix to the perfective verb. The conjugation pattern remains the same.
examples: Neurobím to – I won't do it
Nenapíšeš mi? – Won't you write to me?
Neprečítame knihu – We won't read the book

rules.title: Negation in Future Tense - Imperfective Verbs
rules.text: Add 'ne-' prefix to the auxiliary 'budem' → 'nebudem'. The infinitive remains unchanged.
examples: Nebudem pracovať – I won't be working
Nebudeš čítať? – Won't you be reading?
Nebudeme čakať – We won't be waiting

rules.title: Negation with Reflexive Verbs - Perfective
rules.text: Add 'ne-' prefix to the perfective reflexive verb. The reflexive particle position remains after the verb.
examples: Nenaučím sa to – I won't learn it
Neumyješ sa? – Won't you wash yourself?
Neoblečieme sa – We won't get dressed

rules.title: Negation with Reflexive Verbs - Imperfective
rules.text: Add 'ne-' prefix to the auxiliary 'budem' → 'nebudem'. The reflexive particle and infinitive remain unchanged.
examples: Nebudem sa učiť – I won't be learning
Nebudeš sa umývať? – Won't you be washing?
Nebudú sa obliekať – They won't be getting dressed

rules.title: Irregular Future Forms - 'ísť' (to go)
rules.text: The verb 'ísť' has an irregular perfective future form 'pôjdem' (I will go). This is one of the most common irregular future forms.
examples: Pôjdem domov – I will go home
Pôjdeš do školy? – Will you go to school?
Nepôjdeme na prechádzku – We won't go for a walk
Budú chodiť každý deň – They will go every day (habitual)

rules.title: Irregular Future Forms - 'prísť' (to come)
rules.text: The verb 'prísť' (to come) has irregular future conjugation 'prídem'.
examples: Prídem zajtra – I will come tomorrow
Prídeš na večeru? – Will you come to dinner?
Neprídu na stretnutie – They won't come to the meeting

rules.title: Irregular Future Forms - 'jesť' (to eat)
rules.text: The perfective form 'zjesť' (to eat up) has the future conjugation 'zjem'.
examples: Zjem obed – I will eat lunch
Zješ to všetko? – Will you eat all of it?
Budem jesť pomaly – I will be eating slowly

rules.title: Irregular Future Forms - 'piť' (to drink)
rules.text: The perfective form 'vypiť' (to drink up) has the future conjugation 'vypijem'.
examples: Vypijem kávu – I will drink coffee
Vypiješ čaj? – Will you drink tea?
Budeme piť vodu – We will be drinking water

rules.title: Irregular Future Forms - 'vedieť' (to know)
rules.text: The verb 'vedieť' has future conjugation 'budem vedieť' (imperfective only - knowing is a state, not a completed action).
examples: Budem vedieť odpoveď – I will know the answer
Budeš vedieť slovenčinu? – Will you know Slovak?
Nebudú vedieť nič – They won't know anything

rules.title: Irregular Future Forms - 'môcť' (can/to be able)
rules.text: The verb 'môcť' has future conjugation 'budem môcť'.
examples: Budem môcť prísť – I will be able to come
Budeš môcť pomôcť? – Will you be able to help?
Nebudeme môcť zostať – We won't be able to stay

rules.title: Common Irregular Perfective Future Verbs
rules.text: Several common verbs have irregular perfective future forms that must be memorized.
examples: Pôjdem do obchodu – I will go to the store
Prídu o piatej – They will come at five
Nájdem riešenie – I will find a solution
Vezmem si knihu – I will take a book
